Records.description: Two-loop on-shell sunrise (1 massive, 2 massless) with $p^2=M^2$; master integral for SMEFT top self-energy computation. Closed form from literature.
Records.familyName: two-loop on-shell sunrise with one massive and two massless propagators

Records.description: Two-loop sunset with two massless and one massive internal lines. Two master integrals chosen as pure ${}_2F_1$ functions of $p^2/m^2$. Diagrammatic coaction constructed via the global ${}_2F_1$ coaction; maximal cuts evaluated as a one-dimensional $k_0$ integral.

Records.authors: Gerlach, Marvin, Mishima, Go, Steinhauser, Matthias
Records.description: Two-loop sunrise (banana) integral with one massive and two massless propagators, external momentum $p$ ($p^2=m^2$). Result is exact in epsilon, expressed in terms of Gamma functions. Needed for the scattering channel of the NRQCD matching calculation.

Two-loop sunset graph with three internal propagators, one of which carries mass $m$, and external momentum $p$. The paper defines a seven-argument integral family $I(m^2,p^2,\nu_1,\ldots,\nu_5,D)$ with two irreducible numerators $(k+l)^2$ and $(k+p)^2$, chooses two masters $I^{(1)} = (p^2-m^2) I(m^2,p^2,1,1,1,0,0,2-2\varepsilon)$ and $I^{(2)} = -I(m^2,p^2,1,1,1,1,0,2-2\varepsilon)$, both evaluated in closed form as Gauss ${}_2F_1$ hypergeometric functions of $p^2/m^2$. The maximal cuts are computed by iterated residues / dispersion-like frame parametrization and give two distinct cut contours (second ${}_2F_1$ arguments $1-m^2/p^2$ and $p^2/(p^2-m^2)$); the diagrammatic coaction $\Delta I^{(i)} = \sum_j I^{(j)} \otimes C^{(j)} I^{(i)}$ is established.
Records.familyName: Two-loop sunset with one internal mass
Records.location: Section 3, eq. defining I(m^2,p^2,nu_1..nu_5,D); Section 4, eqs. (Suns1)-(Suns2) with diagrammatic coaction

Names: two-loop on-shell sunrise with one massive and two massless propagators

Results.resultTeXPreview: \frac{m^2}{2 \varepsilon^2}+\frac{-\frac{M^2}{4}+\frac{3 m^2}{2}+m^2 (-\log (m^2))}{\varepsilon}+\left(m^2 H\left(0,1-m^2 M^{-2}\,;1\right)+\frac{1}{2} m^2^2 M^{-2} \log \left(\frac{m^2}{m^2-M^2}\right)-\frac{1}{2} m^2 \log ^2\left(\frac{m^2}{m^2-M^2}\right)+\frac{1}{2} M^2 \log (m^2)-\frac{1}{2} M^2 \log \left(\frac{m^2}{m^2-M^2}\right)-\frac{13 M^2}{8}+\frac{1}{2} m^2 \zeta(2)+\frac{\pi ^2 m^2}{6}+3 m^2+m^2 \log ^2(m^2)-3 m^2 \log (m^2)\right)+O\left(\varepsilon^1\right)
